summary: Route Inventory Parity Tooling - All acceptance criteria PASSED. (1) route_inventory_summary.json contains route_count/v1_count/legacy_count/inventory_hash/compat_required_count, (2) export_route_inventory.py writes to custom paths correctly, (3) check_route_inventory_parity.py reports all_match=True for same code path and correctly detects count drift, (4) --fail-on-mismatch exits 1 on mismatch, (5) existing v1 aliases still work, (6) CI workflow includes all required steps (artifact generation, parity check, diff, PR summary, upload-artifact, PR comment), (7) deterministic inventory generation confirmed.
passed_tests: ✅ pytest test_route_inventory_parity.py: 2/2 passed
✅ pytest test_api_v1_foundation.py: 3/3 passed
✅ pytest test_api_v1_low_risk_rollout.py: 2/2 passed
✅ pytest test_route_inventory_parity_tooling.py: 10/10 passed
✅ export_route_inventory.py generates inventory + summary to custom paths
✅ route_inventory_summary.json contains required fields: route_count=675, v1_count=17, legacy_count=658, inventory_hash (64-char SHA-256)
✅ check_route_inventory_parity.py reports all_match=True for preview/staging/prod generated from same code
✅ check_route_inventory_parity.py detects mismatch when counts drift and reports correctly
✅ --fail-on-mismatch flag returns exit code 1 on mismatch
✅ Text and JSON output formats work correctly for parity report
✅ diff_route_inventory.py CLI works with both text and json formats
✅ Route inventory is deterministic (same hash across multiple runs)
✅ Inventory entries are sorted by (path, method, source)
✅ GET /api/health returns 200 (legacy endpoint still works)
✅ GET /api/v1/health returns 200 (v1 alias still works)
✅ GET /api/system/ping returns 200 (legacy)
✅ GET /api/v1/system/ping returns 200 (v1 alias)
✅ GET /api/public/theme returns 200 (legacy)
✅ GET /api/v1/public/theme returns 200 (v1 alias)
✅ CI ci.yml YAML syntax is valid
✅ CI route-inventory job contains all required steps
✅ CI uploads 6 artifacts: inventory, summary, diff (json+txt), parity (json+txt), pr_summary.md
✅ CI deploy job depends on route-inventory
✅ CI PR comment step is conditional on pull_request event
critical_code_review_comments: route_inventory_summary.py: summarize_route_inventory() correctly computes all counts and SHA-256 hash from inventory
route_inventory_summary.py: build_route_inventory_parity_report() properly compares counts and hashes, reports mismatches
export_route_inventory.py: CLI properly uses argparse with custom paths, creates parent directories
check_route_inventory_parity.py: CLI correctly parses env=path pairs, supports --fail-on-mismatch flag
ci.yml: route-inventory job is properly structured with fetch-depth: 0 for git history
ci.yml: Simulated parity check (same code path) is honestly labeled and will work identically in real runtime
Implementation is honest about actual staging/prod environments not being accessible - tooling is designed for real runtime parity checks later

success_rate.backend: 100% (17/17 pytest tests passed, 6/6 HTTP endpoint tests passed)

context_for_next_testing_agent: Route inventory parity tooling is complete. Key artifacts: (1) route_inventory_summary.json with route_count/v1_count/legacy_count/compat_required_count/inventory_hash, (2) export_route_inventory.py CLI supports custom output paths via --destination and --summary-out, (3) check_route_inventory_parity.py CLI supports env=path pairs, --format (json/text), --fail-on-mismatch, (4) CI ci.yml route-inventory job generates all artifacts and posts PR summary, (5) Deterministic inventory generation confirmed via hash consistency tests.

ci_workflow_steps_verified: Comment route inventory summary on PR
